text.title: PM Tool Selection — Agentic Workflow and Best Practices
text.summary: Recommended agentic subagents, prompt patterns, AI-agent gotchas, and best practices for tool evaluation automation.

section.title: Agentic Evaluation Workflow

rule.statement: Human sign-off is mandatory at three points: requirements sign-off, POC scoring, and final ADR approval — agents must not select the tool unilaterally.
rule.rationale: A multi-year tool commitment is irreversible on the scale of a sprint; decision authority for irreversible choices belongs to humans with full organizational context.

section.p: A requirements-gatherer agent runs async interviews, normalizes responses into a MoSCoW matrix, and a vendor-researcher agent pulls live pricing and feature data. A TCO modeler builds the 3-year model. A human reviews all three before the POC begins.

section.title: Recommended Subagents

list.item: requirements-gatherer — runs structured interview templates (current_state, workflow_needs, team_context, future_needs), extracts must/should/nice into YAML.
vendor-researcher — pulls feature matrices, pricing, SOC2/GDPR posture from vendor docs + G2/Capterra; cites source URL per field; flags data older than 90 days.
tco-modeler — builds 3-year TCO with license, implementation, training, admin, and hidden costs.
adr-drafter — produces ADR-style decision record with evaluation summary and migration plan.

section.reference: Script to snapshot vendor pricing pages for diff-review

example.note: Forces citation per field; fails if data is stale or vendor-supplied without verification:
example.code: You are a vendor-researcher subagent. For each tool in {tools}, fetch: - Current pricing tiers (per-user, per-month, annual discount) - SSO/SAML availability per tier - Native integrations with {required_integrations} - API rate limits and auth model Return JSON conforming to schema {pm_tool_profile.json}. Cite source URL per field. Flag any score where source data is older than 90 days or marked "vendor-supplied".

antipattern.description: LLM confidently asserts vendor features without a citation URL.
antipattern.reason: LLMs hallucinate vendor features; pricing pages change quarterly without announcement. Gate every feature claim on a live-fetched citation; fail closed if no URL is provided.

antipattern.description: Agent scores mobile app quality at 8/10 without testing the app on a physical device.
antipattern.reason: Synthetic mobile UX scores are fiction; outsource physical-device testing to a human evaluator or defer the criterion.

antipattern.description: ADR-drafting agent consistently favours Linear over alternatives.
antipattern.reason: Training data bias from developer Twitter skews toward Linear; force the agent to argue the strongest case for the second-place tool before writing the final recommendation.

list.item: Pricing pages change quietly; cache + diff weekly — do not trust last month's research in scoring.
POC scorecard suffers from the agent flattering the user's apparent preference; randomize tool order in prompts.
Never commit to a multi-year contract from agent output alone; require human sign-off on any annual subscription commitment.

rule.statement: Ask vendors for their churn-back rate (customers who came from a competitor) — this reveals real-world switching satisfaction that G2 star ratings hide.
rule.rationale: Churn-back rate measures the tool's ability to retain customers who evaluated alternatives; it is a stronger signal than aggregate ratings which mix use-case-mismatches with genuine quality issues.

rule.statement: Always negotiate annual + multi-year pricing and ask for "engineering discount" or open-source rate — initial quotes are rarely final.
rule.rationale: PM tool vendors routinely discount 20-40% on multi-year commitments negotiated before renewal; the list price is a ceiling, not a floor.
